blocks.content: Alpheas Magic School (2)

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No, it's not like that. It's just that a good opportunity came up.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Huh? A good opportunity?
Shirone recounted the events that had transpired.
He had already organized the story in his mind, so the words flowed smoothly.
But to the listener, it was nothing short of astonishing.
Vincent's expression gradually turned to shock.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Honestly, at some point, he stopped hearing Shirone's voice.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: He couldn't fathom what his son was saying.
Had his son befriended the youngest son of the Ozent family? And that friend had sworn a knight's oath to him?
Moreover, the family had accepted it and allowed them to remain friends?
With each successive breach of common sense, it felt like thunder was crashing in his head—boom, boom, boom.
When the story finally ended, Vincent silently scratched his head.
He knew his son was clever and deep-thinking.
But even so, this was unbelievable.
Had his son's mind gone astray? Had the hardships here driven him into delusions of grandeur?

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Shirone, be honest with me. Your father would do anything for you. What exactly happened to you? Who did this to you?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's the truth, Father. Why would I lie about something like this?

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Exactly! You're not a liar. So tell me. Who tormented you? Who made your life miserable?
Vincent was genuinely furious.
He had seen countless examples of commoners who got entangled with noble families and came out half-broken.
Even Shirone thought the story was absurd.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But he couldn't think of a better way to convince them than telling the truth.
As he struggled to find the right words, the door to the reception room opened, and the direct family members of the Ozent household entered.
Pleased to meet you. I am the family head, Bishop.

blocks.speaker: Bishop
blocks.content: Bishop, the family head, greeted everyone on behalf of the group.
Though Klump and Rian were absent, Lian and Reina had accompanied them instead.
Lian bowed deeply to Vincent, his heart filled with warmth.

blocks.speaker: Lian
blocks.content: Greetings, Father! I am Rian, Shirone's sword. The future greatest swordsman in the world! Hahahaha!

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: I'm Reina, Rian's older sister. I've heard much about you.
Vincent and Olina could only blink in stunned silence.
Then, as if a circuit had suddenly connected in their minds, they realized the situation and bowed their heads.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Ah, greetings! I'm Shirone's father. To think esteemed individuals like you would come to such a place...

blocks.speaker: Bishop
blocks.content: Oh? Didn't Shirone tell you?

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Tell me? About what?
Vincent racked his brain, but the only thing that came to mind was his son's unbelievable boast.

blocks.speaker: Bishop
blocks.content: So... that story was true?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Ah, I suppose it must be. We might have arrived a bit too early. Anyway, it's exactly as Shirone said.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: My younger brother Rian and Shirone are bound by a knight's oath. That's why we went through the trouble of bringing you here.
Finally grasping the full picture, Vincent's heart pounded.
The joy was fleeting, quickly replaced by fear.
What kind of trouble had his naive son gotten himself into?

blocks.speaker: Bishop
blocks.content: Let's have a meal first. We can discuss the details afterward.
Bishop, true to his character, concluded the matter briefly and turned away.
The family head's attitude wasn't particularly friendly.
But the fact that a noble of the second rank used honorifics with a commoner was telling—it revealed the parents' feelings of having their youngest son taken hostage.
Even after the family members left, Vincent remained seated for a while.
Then, as Shirone tugged at his sleeve with a smile, he snapped out of it and followed him to the living room.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: 'Is this really happening? Our Shirone? With the Ozent family?'

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Everything was a question mark. Every question was a shock.
Olina, trembling like a frightened girl, clung tightly to her husband's arm.
But even Vincent's legs were shaking.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Then he quickly pulled himself together.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: If Shirone's words were true, he couldn't afford to show weakness.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: He had to hold his ground for his son's sake.
What should have been a simple meal felt like a battlefield to Vincent.
A rectangular table was laden with delicacies from land and sea.
Even Vincent, a hunter, saw dishes he had never encountered before.
Some ingredients had been specially procured by Reina from the royal palace.
Seating was arranged according to protocol, with the family head, Bishop, at the head of the table.
To his left sat Klump, and to his right, Reina.
Vincent, seated at the far end, finally felt the reality of the situation as he gazed at the exquisite dishes before him.
When the head butler, Louis, and sub-butler Temuran observed formalities, he unconsciously straightened his shoulders.
But he couldn't simply enjoy the meal.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Considering his son's dignity, he needed to finish the meal quietly.
Yet, the sight of over seven different types of utensils made his head spin.
Commoners could get by with just a fork, even if they were tackling a wild boar.
But the utensils before him varied in size and shape, and some were tools he had never seen before.
The meal began, but Vincent's family still hadn't taken their first bite.
The biggest problem was that the maids merely stood by, making no effort to assist.
In the precarious psychological battle between nobles and commoners, recklessness was unnecessary.
Moreover, commoners who had suddenly risen in status were often met with ridicule and sarcasm.

blocks.speaker: Bishop
blocks.content: Please, eat. There's no need to adhere to noble etiquette.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: B-but still...

blocks.speaker: Bishop
blocks.content: Etiquette isn't about actions but the intent behind them.

blocks.speaker: Bishop
blocks.content: Your current struggle alone shows us enough courtesy. Please, eat comfortably.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Vincent was moved by the family head's words.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Whether it was genuine consideration or noble pride, such attention was a significant gesture.
The maids paled.
The Ozent family's attitude toward Shirone was genuine.
They truly intended to accept him.
Flustered, the maids hastily explained how to use the utensils.
The real issue was ignorance—once they knew, eating wouldn't be so hard.
Vincent's family, having picked up on the cues, finally ate comfortably.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: With his composure restored, Vincent grew concerned about his son.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: What exactly did this family intend to do with Shirone?

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: He had heard the explanation of the knight's oath, but that didn't change anything.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Shirone was still the son of a hunter, and aside from being more clever than most, his future was far from guaranteed.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Was there some ulterior motive?

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: He hated suspecting those who showed kindness, but when it came to his child, Vincent would stop at nothing.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Thank you for looking favorably on my son, but I can't help but worry.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: The Ozent family is one of the most prestigious in Creas, isn't it? It's hard to believe that such a family's young master would swear a knight's oath to Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Bishop
blocks.content: Haha! You needn't worry about that.

blocks.speaker: Bishop
blocks.content: Shirone and I—

blocks.speaker: Bishop
blocks.content: To be honest, I do feel a bit displeased.
The utensils around the table froze.
But Bishop continued cutting his meat without pause.

blocks.speaker: Bishop
blocks.content: However, it's a knight's oath.

blocks.speaker: Bishop
blocks.content: For the military, it's the highest form of binding pride and conviction.

blocks.speaker: Bishop
blocks.content: To harbor ill feelings simply because of a difference in status would tarnish Rian's honor.

blocks.speaker: Bishop
blocks.content: So, we'll continue using honorifics—please don't feel too burdened.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Bishop's words resonated deeply with Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Most nobles were arrogant and self-absorbed.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But precisely because of that, the values they revered surpassed imagination.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Nobles, whom he had always seen as oppressors, were now revealed to be individuals governed by their own codes.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Kukuku, our family head finally speaks sense.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Must be lonely sending off your last remaining son, eh? Why not just send him off next year?

blocks.speaker: Bishop
blocks.content: Rian has mastered the Schema and combined it with Ozent swordsmanship, so his chances of earning recognition are high.

blocks.speaker: Bishop
blocks.content: Shirone, on the other hand, hasn't even grasped the Schema yet.

blocks.speaker: Bishop
blocks.content: We have no choice but to pin our hopes on this swordsmanship school.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Sounds like you're subtly favoring the black-haired one.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Cold-hearted, aren't you?

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: But blue blood runs with undying youth.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Underestimating the passion of the blue-haired might lead to embarrassment later.
Rian, chewing on lamb, gave his grandfather a thumbs-up.
Bishop snorted.
He was truly fed up with his father's antics.

blocks.speaker: Bishop
blocks.content: Blue-haired or black-haired, whoever succeeds is still my child.

blocks.speaker: Bishop
blocks.content: ...Ozent.
After dinner, Klump gathered the family in the living room to discuss Shirone's future.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: Reina shared during the family meeting that Shirone was interested in magic, and everyone agreed.
Given his physique and temperament, swordsmanship had its limits.
In the end, calling Vincent had been the right decision.
Despite the warm atmosphere during dinner, Klump wore a solemn expression as he made the announcement.

blocks.speaker: Klump
blocks.content: As you know, our family intends to support Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Klump
blocks.content: I hear he wants to become a mage, so how about sending him to Alpheas Magic School here in Creas?

blocks.speaker: Klump
blocks.content: Headmaster Alpheas is an old friend of mine, and the school is one of the top five in the Torumia Kingdom.

blocks.speaker: Klump
blocks.content: As a state-designated institution, graduating guarantees an unofficial qualification.

blocks.speaker: Klump
blocks.content: In short, he can become a mage.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: A... a mage?
Vincent swallowed hard.
Mages were the epitome of intellect, rulers of phenomena.
It was a special profession, unrelated to status.
Once certified, they were highly sought-after talents.
Some in the world were self-taught mages, while others learned as apprentices under certified mages.
Such individuals could join mercenary groups or associations to earn their qualifications.
But these cases were extremely rare.
For most commoners, becoming a mage was a distant dream.
Yet now, Shirone could become a mage—and not just any mage, but one from a prestigious school that granted qualifications upon graduation.
As a father, it was an offer he couldn't refuse.
Olina burst into tears, overwhelmed with emotion.
Shirone hugged his mother.
It was a heartwarming scene, but Klump's expression remained dark.
Attending magic school was undoubtedly the fastest path to becoming a mage.
But the problem was Shirone's status.
Magic schools strictly admitted nobles, and special admissions were as rare as plucking stars from the sky.
Even the influence of a second-rank family like the Ozents couldn't easily change that.

blocks.speaker: Klump
blocks.content: You must already know this, but Shirone's current status doesn't meet the school's admission requirements.

blocks.speaker: Klump
blocks.content: While we can cover tuition and material costs, the admission method will inevitably raise questions.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Vincent snapped out of his dream.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: The fantasy had been so sweet that he had momentarily forgotten reality.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Ah, of course. I know. Yes... I see.
Disappointment seeped into Vincent's voice.
There was self-loathing, too.
His son, acknowledged by a noble family, couldn't attend school because of the status inherited from his worthless father.

blocks.speaker: Klump
blocks.content: There is one way. No, it's the only way.
At Klump's words, Vincent's head jerked up.
Shirone, hearing this for the first time, also paid close attention.

blocks.speaker: Klump
blocks.content: We want to adopt... Shirone into the Ozent family.
Silence filled the living room.
Klump's proposal was, in essence, a request to sell his child.
Of course, it wasn't a bad offer—the son of a hunter would instantly become a son of the Ozent family.
Under normal circumstances, such an offer would be unthinkable.
But the reason Vincent hesitated was the matter of blood.
Shirone could become a noble.
But from that moment on, he would no longer be his son.
His child would belong to someone else.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Vincent looked at Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Annoyingly, his son's expression remained calm.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Was it because he assumed it would be accepted, or because he thought it was absurd?

blocks.speaker: Klump
blocks.content: After discussing it as a family, we concluded this is the best solution.

blocks.speaker: Klump
blocks.content: Among our cousins, there's one without children.

blocks.speaker: Klump
blocks.content: Registering Shirone under him would grant him noble status, making school admission possible.

blocks.speaker: Klump
blocks.content: Of course, we have no intention of taking your son away.

blocks.speaker: Klump
blocks.content: It's purely a paperwork matter—family ties won't change.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Just a paperwork matter.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Could it really be that simple?

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: In this world, everything was a paperwork matter.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Vincent wasn't confident.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: He couldn't guarantee he wouldn't feel resentful seeing Shirone registered as another family's son.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Shirone, say something. What should your father decide? What do you truly want?

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Vincent looked at Shirone with a miserable heart.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Shirone frowned.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Vincent realized his son was frustrated by his indecision.
Given the sensitivity of the matter, everyone held their tongues until Reina spoke up.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: Shirone, what do you think? It's just paperwork.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: You can become a noble and achieve your dream.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: Of course, we'll always consider your current parents as your real ones.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: Nothing will change.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone's frown deepened, his eyes narrowing.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Then, as if he couldn't comprehend it, he spoke.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone's voice trailed off, leaving his words hanging in the air.
The room fell silent, waiting for Shirone's response.
